If we consider Persicaria wallichii, here's a paragraph about the plant:

Persicaria wallichii is a perennial herb native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia, belonging to the Polygonaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Persicaria wallichii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Persicaria wallichii is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and ointments for the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
